title: Fix PostgreSQL Image Storage for Branding Favicon and Logo
description:
Saving favicon and logo images to the database from the branding settings page was failing under PostgreSQL. The issue was caused by differences in how PostgreSQL handles binary/image data storage compared to MySQL.

The fix also resolved a secondary bug where the browser page tab title was not updating after being changed in branding settings.
acceptance_criteria: Favicon uploads successfully save to the PostgreSQL database
Logo uploads successfully save to the PostgreSQL database
Branding page tab title updates correctly after saving
Fix is validated on both PostgreSQL (local) and MySQL (staging)
middleware_changes: Updated image save logic to be compatible with PostgreSQL binary data handling for favicon and logo uploads. Fixed page tab title update logic in branding settings.
data_model_changes: Image storage column handling adjusted for PostgreSQL compatibility (binary data encoding differences from MySQL).
implementation_notes: Tested on both PostgreSQL (local development) and MySQL (staging). The fix ensures cross-database compatibility for image storage in the branding module.
comment_summary: Waqar fixed the image storage issue on his local PostgreSQL instance and also resolved a page tab title bug. Tested and confirmed working on both PostgreSQL and MySQL environments.
